title: Union-closed sets conjecture: the 'contains a singleton' special case
informal_statement: The union-closed sets conjecture (Frankl's conjecture): for every finite union-closed family of sets A (other than {empty set}), there exists an element belonging to at least half of the sets in A. This remains open in general. This packet proves the special case where A contains some singleton {i}: then i itself is a witness for the >=1/2 bound. Splits A into B (members not containing i) and C (members containing i); shows 'insert i' injects B into C using union-closedness applied to each member of B together with the witness singleton {i}, giving card(B) <= card(C), hence card(C) >= card(A)/2.
informal_proof_idea: Let B = {x in A : i not in x}, C = {x in A : i in x}. The map x -> insert i x sends B into C (since A is union-closed and {i} in A, x union {i} = insert i x is in A, and it contains i) and is injective (erase i on both sides of an equality insert i x = insert i y recovers x = y using x.erase i = x, y.erase i = y since i is not in x or y). Hence card B <= card C via Finset.card_le_card_of_injOn. Since card C + card B = card A (partition of A by membership of i), card A <= 2 * card C, i.e. (1/2) * card A <= card C.
formal_statement_pp: theorem union_closed_singleton_mem : ∀ {n : Type*} [DecidableEq n] {A : Finset (Finset n)}, (∀ X ∈ A, ∀ Y ∈ A, X ∪ Y ∈ A) → ∀ (i : n), {i} ∈ A → ∃ i, (1 / 2 : ℚ) * (Finset.card A) ≤ Finset.card (Finset.filter (fun x => i ∈ x) A)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves only the 'A contains a singleton' special case of the union-closed sets conjecture, not the conjecture in general (open in this corpus and in the literature -- the upstream file's own `union_closed` theorem for the general case is `sorry`/`research open`, and the best known general bound is Yu 2023's ~0.38234 constant, not the conjectured 1/2). Found during round 8's `Wikipedia/` bulk-grep triage: `UnionClosed.lean` (tags=6, sorries=5) had sorries strictly less than tags, flagging at least one solved theorem as sorry-free -- confirmed 3 of the 6 tagged theorems are genuinely proof-complete (`univ_card_two`, `singleton_mem`, `sharpness`); this packet transports `singleton_mem` (the other two are candidates for future cycles). Restated (not verbatim) in two respects: (1) the upstream file's `IsUnionClosed` is a transparent Prop `abbrev`, inlined directly as `∀ X ∈ A, ∀ Y ∈ A, X ∪ Y ∈ A` rather than re-declared as a def, since it carries no information beyond that formula; (2) the upstream proof's `#A`/`{x ∈ A | i ∈ x}` notations are `scoped` behind `open Finset` (confirmed by grepping the vendored Mathlib source directly for `scoped prefix ... "#"`), which `SubmitModule` items cannot include, so both were spelled out as plain `Finset.card`/`Finset.filter` applications -- the proof term is otherwise verbatim and required no tactic-level changes. Kernel-verified on the FIRST attempt (episode ff2e3924-a37c-44aa-9c0d-b60e3bf4e81f), using `proof_format: raw_lean_block` for this root theorem since the proof is a genuinely sequential, bullet-free multi-`have` script needing no rewriting for the flattener. `training.eligibility: quarantined` per this session's default policy for adapted/restated frontier packets; `open_problem_related: true` since this is one special case of a conjecture that remains fully open in general.
